data:
Versatile and technology-agnostic programmer passionate about creating meaningful applications and tools.
With expertise in back-end development, I am committed to delivering exceptional user experiences, be it through full-featured web applications, sophisticated desktop interfaces, or streamlined command line tools.
Engaged in a groundbreaking Telemetry and Observability project
Developping a Big Data system in Rust with Parquet, Arrow, and DataFusion
Conducting exploratory work in Python and Jupyter as a preliminary step before implementing the solution in Rust
Prioritizing performance and optimization across the project spectrum
Crafting high-performance online services and APIs in Rust using Axum
Writing and improving highly optimized SQL queries on tens of millions of records
Driving frontend architecture with TypeScript and Svelte
Key role in a Grafana plugin development written in Go
Developed a low-level Rust Wasm plugin, enabling web applications to seamlessly transmit thousands of events per frame to the Telemetry tool
Participated in the design and implementation of a robust infrastructure using AWS, GCP, and Terraform

Thrilled to contribute to the dynamic Haven Studios team and be part of the broader Sony family.
Currently, I am focused on an ambitious Telemetry project, leveraging Rust and TypeScript to elevate user experience and optimize performance of all within Haven.
Contributed to a cutting-edge tech stack, utilizing Rust, TypeScript, and Python
Led design and development of a game engine GUI app with TypeScript, Tauri, and Rust for a hybrid desktop-web experience
Played a key role in server-side development with Rust, contributing to Rest and gRPC services using Axum
Engineered advanced command line applications, including code generators and NPM tooling, all in Rust
Integral part of the Telemetry project, focusing on performance analysis and optimization in Rust

My tenure with Legion Labs was not just professionally thrilling but also profoundly uplifting. At the heart of our dynamic projects was a robust and cutting-edge technology stack, featuring Rust, TypeScript, and Python.
In a pivotal role, I led the architecture and development of the Editor UI, crafted in TypeScript and Svelte, with Rust and Tauri at its core.
Simultaneously, I played a crucial role in a Telemetry project, focusing on performance analysis - a pursuit that remains central to my work at Haven Studios.
Beyond UI and tools, my responsibilities extended to making significant contributions to server-side development and driving enhancements in our tooling landscape.
Encourage Project: Led both front-end development and served as the Scrum Master, ensuring project efficiency and collaboration
Mikketa Project: Directed front-end development, showcasing sustained leadership and expertise
ATS Project: Transitioned to a full-stack developer role, leading the entire development process
Designed and implemented critical APIs in Rust

Embarking as the lead front-end developer, my tenure at Scoville has been characterized by continuous growth and escalating responsibilities. Eager to contribute more, I evolved into the role of web team lead and later assumed the position of a full-stack developer on the ATS project (Applicant Tracking System).
Throughout these roles, I maintained a strong commitment to team collaboration. My leadership style places a high value on collective success, reflecting not only personal growth but also a dedication to delivering impactful solutions and leading high-performing teams.
Orchestrated the overall technical management of the ATS project
Defined the Jira flow, wrote user stories, and meticulously maintained the backlog and Jira board for streamlined project development
Designed the entire stack for both front-end and back-end development, showcasing a comprehensive grasp of project architecture in its entirety
Implemented features in TypeScript, with React and Next.js

I collaborated closely with the business team, delving into discussions to comprehend, analyze, and articulate project requirements.
My involvement extended beyond discussions - I actively contributed to the development process, taking a hands-on approach in bootstrapping both the front-end and back-end codebases.
Designed and implemented the frontend stack using TypeScript, React, ReScript, and GraphQL
Proactively engaged with the business team, refining the feature set and discussing project requirements
Led a team of four as the tech and front-end lead, making crucial technology decisions and actively participating in functional meetings
Implemented critical features, conducted in-depth code reviews, and successfully onboarded new team members
Applied Domain Driven Design (DDD) principles and Agile methodologies to enhance development processes

As the appointed tech lead, with a specific focus on leading the front-end, I managed a dynamic team of four, making critical decisions on technology stack selection and overseeing feature implementation.
My role extended beyond traditional leadership to active involvement in functional meetings, including pre-groomings and groomings. This hands-on participation allowed me to contribute significantly to defining the feature set and collaboratively find straightforward solutions to address client pain points.
Led Agile methodology adoption for streamlined development and enhanced team communication
Oversaw four web applications, including sales-focused tools and the main website
Implemented client-side features in TypeScript and React to improve user experiences
Facilitated smoother team communication for efficient collaboration
Designed and developed a Rust-based "Rest to GraphQL" API for improved efficiency and type safety

I led the En-Courage project with a team of three developers, overseeing two sales applications, a Customer Service Management (CSM) tool, and the main website http://en-courage.com. Introducing Agile methodologies, I optimized scheduling and team communication.
For development, we used React, Redux, and RxJS in TypeScript for the front end, with Go for backend APIs. To enhance communication, a Rust-built GraphQL abstraction server was implemented, ensuring improved type safety without creating bottlenecks.
Worked as a full-stack developer/consultant
Developed and delivered training sessions in React and Node
Actively participated in the web department development with the "Experts Team"

Joined Soat as a junior front-end developer, later advancing to a lead full-stack developer and tech trainer.
Authored and delivered two extensive training sessions, specializing in React and Node, for major companies.
This experience provided a remarkable opportunity to share insights, connect with diverse audiences, and gain valuable knowledge by exploring various companies' technical needs and analyzing their requirements.
Architected the front-end using TypeScript, React, Redux, and RxJS
Implemented new features to enhance functionality
Contributed to the CI/CD pipeline using CircleCI
Authored AWS templates using CloudFormation
Engaged in planning meetings alongside the Product Owner and the Swiss Knife team
Assisted in the development of automated tests using Katalon
BNP IFS (International Financial Services) initiated six new projects, and I had the privilege of joining one of these teams as a tech lead, embracing a new and exciting challenge.
Implemented new features using React with TypeScript
Contributed to back-end development using C#

I had the opportunity to contribute to a substantial application facilitating foreign currency exchange.
In a team of 10, my responsibilities included developing new features in both React/TypeScript for the front-end and C# for the back-end.
Led and developed the in-house RTE project, a React/Draft.js tool written in TypeScript used by all the writers at Radio France
Introduced TypeScript to the team
Worked on a full-featured Node server, utilizing Express
Utilized SVG in PureScript and Pux to construct Graph tools
Refactored multiple projects, emphasizing TypeScript, to enhance maintainability and efficiency
In a collaborative team of five at Radio France, I played a pivotal role in developing and maintaining multiple applications, primarily focusing on front-end development.
Lead full-stack web developer - Lead font-end developer
Worked on two distinct projects: the "TF1 backoffice" application and the "LCI backoffice" application
Led a team of 5 on the TF1 project
Participated in developments using JavaScript with the AngularJS (1.x) framework
Implemented a robust tag system linking various resources utilized by the front-end website
Developed a full-featured Rich Text Editor (RTE) using Scribe
Contributed to the core API development using Hapi on Node
Engaged in isomorphic development with React and Redux on the front-end and Express on the back-end for the LCI project
Constructed a second RTE using React and Draft.js

Within the context of a comprehensive application refactoring, I joined a team of 5 developers at TF1.
Subsequently, I worked on the "core application," a Node API built using HAPI, for three months.
In the latter part of my mission at TF1, I played a key role in determining the stack and architecture, leading the development of a new application in a team of 4.
Conducted HTML/CSS integration for Tibanne's VPS hosting website
Created new pages and dynamic forms to enhance user interaction
Single-handedly developed the entire front-end for Tibanne's VPS hosting website, covering HTML/CSS integration, dynamic form creation, newsletter email development, and JavaScript optimization.
Wrote "Functional JavaScript", a functional programming library in JavaScript for educational purposes
Created the Mango project, an ORM for MongoDB in Clojure
Developed a GMail POP3 client in Rust
Built several small applications in Node

For one year, I pursued self-directed learning to explore new languages and enhance my expertise in both front-end and back-end development.
This period allowed me to delve into server development using Ruby, Python, and Node. It provided a valuable opportunity to comprehend JavaScript thoroughly, independent of frameworks.
Additionally, I explored new paradigms with languages like Haskell and Scala, contributing to my ongoing quest for better application development practices.
Developed server-side features using Ruby on Rails
Engaged in front-end development with jQuery
Wrote MySQL migrations to manage database changes
Conducted unit testing with RSpec for quality assurance

Contributed to the success of QuelleEnergie, an energy-saving advice company, by actively participating in the development of their website and tools. Leveraged Ruby on Rails and jQuery to implement server-side features and facilitate front-end development.
Additionally, played a key role in analyzing business requirements, writing technical specifications, and ensuring database integrity through MySQL migrations and RSpec unit testing.
Rust services and APIs (Actix, Axum, Juniper, Tokio)
Node (Express, Fastify, Prisma, Nestjs, Hapi, Koa)
AWS (Cognito, ECS, Fargate, Lambda, etc...)
Application Setup (Webpack, Vite, Prettier, ESLint, etc...)
Languages used for fun and profit
